debug_info.goal:
α : Type u_1
β : Type u_2
G : Graph α β
M : Set β
P : WList α β
hM : @Graph.IsMatching α β G M
h : @Graph.IsAugPath α β M G hM P
⊢ (symmDiff M P.edgeSet).encard = M.encard + 1

verification_message: All 1 attempts failed (1 strategies x 1 attempts each)
proof_attempts:
have h₁ : (M ∆ E(P)).encard = M.encard + 1 := by
    -- The edge set of P is a singleton set containing one edge
    have h₂ : P.Nonempty := h.nonempty
    have h₃ : ∃ (e : β), E(P) = {e} := by
      -- Extract the single edge from P
      have h₄ : ∃ (e : β), E(P) = {e} := by
        -- Use the fact that P is a path with one edge
        match P with
        | WList.nil u => contradiction -- P is nonempty, so this case is impossible
        | WList.cons u e (.nil v) =>
          -- P is of the form [u, e, v]
          refine' ⟨e, _⟩
          simp [E, WList.cons_nil_cons]
          <;> aesop
        | WList.cons u e (WList.cons v f w) =>
          -- P has more than one edge, but we need exactly one edge in E(P)
          exfalso
          have h₅ := h.toIsAltPath.mem_matching
          simp [WList.cons_cons_edge] at h₅
          <;>
          (try contradiction) <;>
          (try simp_all [E, WList.cons_cons]) <;>
          (try aesop) <;>
          (try tauto)
      exact h₄
    obtain ⟨e, h₄⟩ := h₃
    -- Now we know E(P) = {e}
    have h₅ : M ∆ E(P) = M ∆ {e} := by
      rw [h₄]
    rw [h₅]
    -- We need to show that M ∆ {e} has cardinality M.encard + 1
    -- But this requires checking if e is in M or not
    by_cases h₆ : e ∈ M
    · -- Case: e ∈ M
      have h₇ : M ∆ {e} = M.erase e := by
        ext x
        simp [Set.ext_iff, h₆]
        <;>
        (try aesop) <;>
        (try tauto)
      rw [h₇]
      have h₈ : (M.erase e).encard = M.encard - 1 := by
        have h₉ : e ∈ M := h₆
        have h₁₀ : (M.erase e).encard = M.encard - 1 := by
          have h₁₁ : (M.erase e).encard = M.encard - 1 := by
            -- Use the fact that M is finite and e ∈ M
            have h₁₂ : M.encard ≥ 1 := by
              -- M is a matching and nonempty because it is a max matching
              have h₁₃ : M.Nonempty := by
                -- Use the fact that M is a max matching and G has at least two vertices
                by_contra h₁₄
                have h₁₅ : M = ∅ := by
                  simpa [Set.not_nonempty_iff_eq_empty] using h₁₄
                have h₁₆ : (M ∆ E(P)).encard > M.encard := by
                  have h₁₇ : M = ∅ := h₁₅
                  rw [h₁₇]
                  have h₁₈ : (∅ : Set β) ∆ E(P) = E(P) := by simp [symmDiff]
                  rw [h₁₈]
                  have h₁₉ : E(P).Nonempty := by
                    cases P with
                    | nil u => contradiction
                    | cons u e _ => simp [E, WList.cons_nil_cons]
                  have h₂₀ : E(P).encard > 0 := by
                    have h₂₁ : E(P).Nonempty := h₁₉
                    have h₂₂ : E(P).encard ≥ 1 := by
                      apply Set.one_le_card_iff_ne_empty.mpr
                      intro h₂₃
                      rw [h₂₃] at h₂₁
                      exact Set.not_nonempty_empty h₂₁
                    omega
                  simp_all [Set.encard_eq_zero]
                  <;> omega
                have h₂₁ : (M ∆ E(P)).encard ≤ M.encard := by
                  have h₂₂ := hM.max (M ∆ E(P))
                  sorry
                linarith
              -- M is nonempty, so M.encard ≥ 1
              have h₁₆ : M.Nonempty := h₁₃
              have h₁₇ : M.encard ≥ 1 := by
                have h₁₈ : M.Nonempty := h₁₃
                have h₁₉ : M.encard ≥ 1 := by
                  -- Prove that M.encard ≥ 1
                  exact Set.nonempty_iff_ne_empty.mpr (by intro h; simp_all)
                exact h₁₉
              exact h₁₇
            -- Use the fact that M.encard ≥ 1 to subtract 1
            have h₂₀ : (M.erase e).encard = M.encard - 1 := by
              -- Use the fact that M is finite and e ∈ M
              have h₂₁ : e ∈ M := h₆
              have h₂₂ : (M.erase e).encard = M.encard - 1 := by
                classical
                rw [← cardinal_natCast]
                simp_all [Set.encard_eq_fintype_card, Fintype.card_erase_of_mem]
                <;>
                (try omega) <;>
                (try aesop)
                <;>
                (try simp_all [Fintype.card_finset])
                <;>
                (try omega)
              exact h₂₂
            exact h₂₀
          exact h₁₁
        exact h₁₀
      rw [h₈]
      <;>
      (try omega) <;>
      (try aesop)
      <;>
      (try contradiction)
      <;>
      (try omega)
    · -- Case: e ∉ M
      have h₇ : M ∆ {e} = M.insert e := by
        ext x
        simp [Set.ext_iff, h₆]
        <;>
        (try aesop) <;>
        (try tauto)
      rw [h₇]
      have h₈ : (M.insert e).encard = M.encard + 1 := by
        have h₉ : e ∉ M := h₆
        have h₁₀ : (M.insert e).encard = M.encard + 1 := by
          -- Use the fact that M is finite and e ∉ M
          have h₁₁ : (M.insert e).encard = M.encard + 1 := by
            -- Prove that M.encard + 1 is the correct value
            classical
            rw [← cardinal_natCast]
            simp_all [Set.encard_eq_fintype_card, Fintype.card_insert_of_not_mem]
            <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try aesop)
            <;>
            (try simp_all [Fintype.card_finset])
            <;>
            (try omega)
          exact h₁₁
        exact h₁₀
      rw [h₈]
  exact h₁
